score_note: Gets the cell/room/door cardinality hierarchy right, but states dominance imprecisely.
Correctly connects minima, image representation, and cardinality control.
Distinguishes point insertion and color insertion, but does not explain the incidence-counting role.
Explains image, missing color, and typed missing color, but only gestures at counting rather than the fixed-type parity role.
Identifies room_of_colorful as prerequisite structure for picking a point, but misses the nonempty/cardinality sandwich.
Recognizes door-room incidences and outside/internal parity, but misses the non-colorful-room side and colorful-room oddness.
Correctly identifies the singleton outside-door filter and odd cardinality.
Mentions odd/even parity and existence, but does not state the exact colorful-room count forced to be odd and nonempty.
States the colorful-cell conclusion, but gives the wrong reason for Inhabited I and misses the default type in typed_colorful_room_odd.
Correctly explains Fcolor on grids, Scarf colorful rooms, room_seq, and room_point_seq.
Understands finite-image subsequence extraction, but treats the stabilized object as a coloring rather than the room color set C.
Correctly says outside-C coordinates tend to zero, but misses how this pairs with coordinate inequalities to force equality.
Identifies compactness as giving a convergent subsequence, but misstates vanishing diameter as directly giving a fixed point.
Captures the broad finite-to-limit bridge, but does not clearly explain the per-color witness and transfer of x_i <= f(x)_i.
Gives the top-level limiting strategy, but misses Scarf/coloring details and treats key lemmas too generically.
Explains deficit/tPush/pushTowardsZ as a retraction device, but does not clearly state positivity of block sums for normalization.
States projection-after-embedding identity, but misses the exact lifted-map and project-back mechanism.
Mentions f_lifted and continuity, but incorrectly describes applying Brouwer in product space and omits project_embed_id at the endpoint.
Mentions finite games and mixed_g, but misses mixedS structure and the unilateral-deviation Nash condition.
Explains normalization and continuity for fixed points, but does not clearly identify payoff-improvement weights.
Connects Brouwer.mixedGame and fixed point broadly, but misses profitable pure deviations and mixed_g_linear.
Gets the two-rooms-even intuition, but misses the fiber-over-door packaging in dbcountingset.
Correctly states the typed nearly colorful versus colorful dichotomy with the same missing type.
Identifies two doors and evenness, but does not explain the two-element fiber in dbcountingset.
Correctly describes TT finite grids, division by l, and indexed orders for Scarf.
Recognizes coordinate bounds and diameter control, but misstates the roles of size_bound_in/out and misses outside-C vanishing.
Identifies a constant-C subsequence, but misses the outside-C and inside-C coordinate estimates that require stabilization.
Names the final coordinate lemmas, but misstates outside-C zero and does not cleanly explain the sum-one/extensionality argument.
Explains flat-to-block indexing, but misses inverse lemmas and sum-changing as the key formal role.
Connects scaling to blockWeight and project_embed_id, but misses the sum-to-one preservation argument.
Correctly identifies division by blockSum, positivity/nonzero denominator, and continuity of projection.
Recognizes the case split for positivity, but gets the tPush = 0 branch wrong and misses zero deficit.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as a weighted-sum reduction from mixed deviations to pure deviations.
Captures representation transport broadly, but omits Fintype.equivFin, map_simplex, and the precise ProductSimplices shape.
Correctly explains nonnegative g_function values, denominator at least one, normalization, and nash_map_cert.
Mentions self_div_lemma and no pure deviations, but misses the sum_g > 1 versus fixed-point normalization contradiction.
Correctly characterizes outside doors as empty tau with singleton color and explains uniqueness for counting.
Correctly explains that the two-room incidence becomes a size-two fiber and hence an even count.
Correctly combines outside-door-to-nearly-colorful and adjacent-room classification into the typed parity setup.
Confuses an odd filtered set with an element of colorful; misses positive cardinality, nonemptiness, choice, and default.
Correctly explains stdSimplex.pick selecting a coordinate/color with x_i <= f(x)_i.
Mentions fixed C and subsequence, but misses outside-C vanishing and inside-C coordinate inequalities.
Identifies y_seq as carrying finite inequalities through diameter and continuity, but misses the colored-witness role and z-specific limit.
Mentions outside-C zero, nonnegativity, and sum one, but does not explain how they force outside mass zero and coordinate equality.
Misdescribes C as a compact subset and says z lies outside C; misses the inside/outside coordinate split.
Correctly explains why the tPush = 0 branch needs zero deficit and blockWeight positivity.
Correctly identifies the blockSum denominator, continuity, and nonzero/positive denominator problem.
Mentions block sums matching block weights and identity, but does not clearly state deficit zero, push identity, and recovery of y.
Explains BigSimplex, projection back, and project_embed_id, but omits the lifted map f_lifted.
Correctly identifies positive payoff improvement from a pure deviation, but omits normalization into nash_map weights.
Explains positive denominator and nonnegative normalized coordinates, but omits nash_map_cert and the explicit sum-one proof.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as a weighted-sum bridge from pure to mixed deviations.
Correctly states the contradiction between H1's sum_g > 1 and self_div_lemma forcing sum_g = 1 at a fixed point.
Correctly explains reindexing via phi/phi_inv into ProductSimplices to apply Brouwer_Product and transport the fixed point back.
